questions.question: The graph of a polynomial $p(x)$ intersects the $x$-axis at exactly $3$ distinct points. What is the number of zeroes of the polynomial $p(x)$?

steps: Recall the geometrical meaning of the zeroes of a polynomial $p(x)$.
The zeroes of a polynomial $p(x)$ are the $x$-coordinates of the points where the graph of $y = p(x)$ intersects the $x$-axis.
Since the graph intersects the $x$-axis at $3$ distinct points, there are exactly $3$ distinct $x$-coordinates where the polynomial value is zero.
Therefore, the number of zeroes is $3$.
solution.derivation: $ \text{Number of zeroes} = \text{Number of intersection points with the } x\text{-axis} \\ \text{Given points of intersection} = 3 \\ \therefore \text{Number of zeroes} = 3$
